User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user profile in the application.

preferences.description: User's preferences (e.g., UI settings, notification preferences). Stored as a JSON string.

profilePhotoUrl.description: URL of the user's profile photo stored in Cloud Storage.

title.description: The title or name of the resume (e.g., 'Software Engineer Resume').

content.description: Resume content, stored as a JSON string.

lastModifiedDate.description: Date the resume was last modified

description.description: A description of the template's style and features.

styling.description: Styling information for the template (e.g., CSS rules), stored as a JSON string.

layout.description: Layout information for the template (e.g., section arrangements), stored as a JSON string.

imageUrl.description: URL of the template's preview image.

Feedback.title: Feedback
Feedback.description: Represents user feedback and collaboration comments.

id.description: Unique identifier for the feedback entry.

timestamp.description: Timestamp of when the feedback was submitted.

definition.description: Stores user profiles, subscription status, and preferences.  User ID is used for path-based ownership of user-specific data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

definition.description: Stores resume data for a specific user. Path-based ownership is enforced via the /users/{userId} path. Denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user (owner of the resume).

params.name: resumeId
params.description: The unique identifier for the resume.

definition.description: Stores available resume templates. These are globally accessible, so no specific authorization fields are needed.

params.name: templateId
params.description: The unique identifier for the template.

definition.description: Stores user feedback and collaboration comments related to a specific resume. Path-based ownership is enforced via the /users/{userId} path. Denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user (owner of the feedback).

params.name: feedbackId
params.description: The unique identifier for the feedback entry.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to ensure data ownership and access control.  The primary strategy is to leverage path-based ownership for user-specific data (resumes, feedback) stored under `/users/{userId}`. This approach simplifies security rules and avoids complex authorization logic. Templates are stored in a top-level collection as they are global and not user-specific.

Authorization Independence is achieved by using path-based ownership (`/users/{userId}/resumes/{resumeId}`) for resumes and feedback, eliminating the need for `get()` calls in security rules. User roles (free/premium) are stored directly in the user document. This denormalization strategy ensures atomic operations.

To support required QAPs:

*   **List Resumes:** Listing resumes is secured via path-based ownership.  A user can only list resumes under their own `/users/{userId}/resumes` collection.
*   **List Feedback:**  Listing feedback is also secured via path-based ownership at `/users/{userId}/feedback`. Only the associated user has read access.
*   **Templates:** Templates are public, therefore they are kept in a separate top level collection, that does not require any authorization.
